tossups.question: After the unveiling of the design of one of these buildings, one newspaper described it as an “architectural King Kong.” Multicoloured glass panels adorn all four walls of one of these buildings in Málaga, which was originally called El Cubo (“KOO-boh”). A paper tube studio used to design one of these buildings was incorporated into the final design; the design for the roof of that one of these buildings was inspired by a Chinese hat found by Shigeru Ban. Sixteen sculptures by (*) Niki de Saint-Phalle (“sang-FAHL”) and Jean Tinguely (“zhon tang-GLEE”) form the Stravinsky Fountain near one of these buildings. Colour-coded pipe systems feature on the exterior of that one of these buildings, whose design team included Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano (“RENT-so P’YAH-no”). For 10 points, name these buildings such as the Centre Pompidou (“sont POM-pee-doo”), which has the largest modern collection in Europe.
tossups.question_sanitized: After the unveiling of the design of one of these buildings, one newspaper described it as an "architectural King Kong." Multicoloured glass panels adorn all four walls of one of these buildings in Malaga, which was originally called El Cubo ("KOO-boh"). A paper tube studio used to design one of these buildings was incorporated into the final design; the design for the roof of that one of these buildings was inspired by a Chinese hat found by Shigeru Ban. Sixteen sculptures by (*) Niki de Saint-Phalle ("sang-FAHL") and Jean Tinguely ("zhon tang-GLEE") form the Stravinsky Fountain near one of these buildings. Colour-coded pipe systems feature on the exterior of that one of these buildings, whose design team included Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano ("RENT-so P'YAH-no"). For 10 points, name these buildings such as the Centre Pompidou ("sont POM-pee-doo"), which has the largest modern collection in Europe.
tossups.answer: art museums [or art galleries; accept Centre Pompidou before mention; accept Pompidou-Málaga or Pompidou-Metz]
tossups.answer_sanitized: art museums [or art galleries; accept Centre Pompidou before mention; accept Pompidou-Malaga or Pompidou-Metz]

tossups.question: In a project from an institution with this name, OMA will add a rooftop garden to an old industrial building in Jersey City. Another building with this name features an undulating roof of glue-laminated timber, inspired by a Chinese hat and designed by Shigeru Ban. Somber black and whimsical colorful sculptures by Jean Tinguely and Niki de Saint-Phalle stand in the (*) Stravinsky Fountain next to a building with this name. Pedestrians can traverse the floors of a building with this name using an escalator marked in red across its front façade. The exterior of that building with this name features blue, yellow, and green pipes color-coded by utility. For 10 points, give this name of a Parisian museum designed by Renzo Piano.
tossups.answer: Centre Pompidou [accept Center Pompidou-Metz; accept Centre Pompidou x Jersey City]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Centre Pompidou [accept Center Pompidou-Metz; accept Centre Pompidou x Jersey City]
tossups.question_sanitized: In a project from an institution with this name, OMA will add a rooftop garden to an old industrial building in Jersey City. Pedestrians can traverse the floors of a building with this name using an escalator marked in red across its front facade. The exterior of that building with this name features blue, yellow, and green pipes color-coded by utility. For 10 points, give this name of a Parisian museum designed by Renzo Piano.

tossups.question: Along with the Centre PHI, Guy Maddin’s film The Forbidden Room was shot in public studios in this building. Gordon Matta-Clark made Conical Intersect to critique the perceived gentrification caused by the construction of this building. The design for this building was inspired by Cedric Price’s Fun Palace and architecture firms such as Archigram and Superstudio. The architects of this building later designed an extension to it for IRCAM. Jean Tinguely [“tahn-guh-LEE”] and Niki de Saint-Phalle designed sculptures for the (*) Stravinsky Fountain found outside of this museum. This building has an external escalator surrounded by a glass tube to go between floors. Color-coded pipes line the outside of this building that houses the Musée National d'Art Moderne. For 10 points, name this Paris building designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Centre Georges Pompidou [or Centre Pompidou; or Pompidou Center] <ES>
tossups.question_sanitized: Along with the Centre PHI, Guy Maddin's film The Forbidden Room was shot in public studios in this building. The design for this building was inspired by Cedric Price's Fun Palace and architecture firms such as Archigram and Superstudio. The architects of this building later designed an extension to it for IRCAM. Jean Tinguely ["tahn-guh-LEE"] and Niki de Saint-Phalle designed sculptures for the (*) Stravinsky Fountain found outside of this museum. This building has an external escalator surrounded by a glass tube to go between floors. Color-coded pipes line the outside of this building that houses the Musee National d'Art Moderne. For 10 points, name this Paris building designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano.

tossups.question: This structure is notoriously called a “Trojan Horse” in Sanford Kwinter’s book Requiem: For the City at the End of the Millennium. To help engineer this masterpiece, 9-ton, cast-steel “gerberettes” were used to support its long trusses. Along with a building in New York City, Nam June Paik broadcasted Good Morning, Mr. Orwell from this building. Jean Tinguely’s kinetic sculptures of a firebird and a fox can be found at Stravinsky Fountain, which is nearby this building that was inspired by Archigram’s “Plug-in City.” This building, which houses a library, a medical center, and an art museum, contains no internal elevators. Instead, a large escalator attached to the front façade moves people along its seven floors. Giant blue tubes carry oxygen into this building, which gets its water from green tubes and electricity from yellow tubes. For 10 points, name this Parisian building that was designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano, and named for a Prime Minister of France.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Pompidou Center [or Centre Georges Pompidou; or Centre Pompidou]

bonuses.leadin: Two answers required. A building designed by one of these two architects, the Lloyd’s Building, sits across the street from the other’s Willis Building. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Two answers required. A building designed by one of these two architects, the Lloyd's Building, sits across the street from the other's Willis Building. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these pioneers of high-tech architecture who met at Yale. These two architects and their future spouses, Su Brumwell and Wendy Cheesman, founded the firm Team 4, which designed the Reliance Controls factory.
Both the Lloyd’s Building and the Willis Building are in this city, where Foster later designed The Gherkin.
After separating from Foster, Rogers collaborated with this architect on a building whose exterior is covered with color-coded tubes. This architect also designed the tallest building in London.
parts_sanitized: Name these pioneers of high-tech architecture who met at Yale. These two architects and their future spouses, Su Brumwell and Wendy Cheesman, founded the firm Team 4, which designed the Reliance Controls factory.
Both the Lloyd's Building and the Willis Building are in this city, where Foster later designed The Gherkin.
After separating from Foster, Rogers collaborated with this architect on a building whose exterior is covered with color-coded tubes. This architect also designed the tallest building in London.
answers: Renzo Piano (The buildings are the Centre Pompidou and The Shard.)
answers_sanitized: Renzo Piano (The buildings are the Centre Pompidou and The Shard.)

bonuses.leadin: Large-scale sculptures of women by this artist include one named "Hon," which contained a theater, fish pond, and sandwich vending machine. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Large-scale sculptures of women by this artist include one named "Hon," which contained a theater, fish pond, and sandwich vending machine. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who created many colorful paper-mache sculptures of voluminous women called "Nanas."
A 1963 work by Saint Phalle consisting of pink paint drops with fake flowers and plastic figures is titled "My heart belongs to [this artist]." This artist created readymades like Fountain.

Saint Phalle collaborated with her husband Jean Tinguley on the Stravinsky Fountain, an artwork made for this museum. This museum includes a curving fiberglass roof designed by Shigeru Ban and prominent color-coded pipes.

bonuses.leadin: In the 1960s, this land artist created multiple series of holes, such as one set titled North, East, South, West. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In the 1960s, this land artist created multiple series of holes, such as one set titled North, East, South, West. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who dug two massive trenches in the Moapa Valley for his piece Double Negative. This artist of City is one of the preeminent living land artists alongside James Turrell.
In 1975, the artist Gordon Matta-Clark bored a conical hole into the walls of two historic buildings that were set to be demolished during the construction of this contemporary art museum in Paris.

This artist cut a hole in the ceiling of an apartment in an installation about a man who fired himself into space using a homemade catapult. Escape was a recurring theme for this Soviet-born artist, whose wife described his pieces as “little heavens we make in our larger hells.”

bonuses.leadin: This artist cut up the façade of a condemned house in Niagara Falls’s Love Canal neighborhood before moving the rest to its Artpark in the work Bingo. For 10 points each:

For Conical Intersect, Matta-Clark cut two townhouses that were to be demolished to make room for this art museum. The exterior of this Parisian modern art museum is lined with color-coded pipes.

Matta-Clark's theory of “anarchitecture” was influenced by this artist’s theories of entropy. This land artist, whom Matta-Clark met at Cornell’s “Earth Art” exhibition, created Partially Buried Woodshed at Kent State.

bonuses.leadin: Alexander Calder’s sculpture Horizontal stands in front of this building. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building designed by Piano, Rogers, and Francini that has color-coded piping and steel structural elements on its outside.

The Centre Pompidou is in this city whose Musée d’Orsay (“myoo-ZAY dor-SAY”) was converted from a Beaux-Arts (“boh-ZARR”) train station. Another museum in this city includes an I. M. Pei-designed glass pyramid complex.
A branch of the Pompidou Arts Centre in this French city was designed by Japanese architect Shigeru Ban. Its complex undulating roof was inspired by a Chinese hat that the architect found in Paris.
answers_sanitized: Centre Pompidou [or Centre Georges Pompidou; or Pompidou Center]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Jean Pigozzi’s collection of African art, the largest such collection in the world, for 10 points each.
parts: The collection contains many paintings by Meleko Mokgosi, an artist who teaches at this US university’s Gallatin School. Much of Randy Weston’s African-inspired music, including An African Nubian Suite, was first performed at this university in Greenwich Village.

Pigozzi was inspired to assemble his collection after viewing the 1989 Magiciens de la terre (“ma-zheese-YAN duh la TAIR”) exhibition at this Parisian building designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano, which features many colored tubes.

Pigozzi’s collection includes works by Chéri Samba, an artist from this African country who appears in Mwezé Ngangura’s film Kin Kiesse (“keen kees”). This country was home to Papa Wemba, who blended a number of Latin influences to create this country’s namesake “rumba” style.
answers: Democratic Republic of the Congo [or DRC or Zaire; prompt on Congo; do not accept or prompt on “Republic of the Congo”]
answers_sanitized: Centre Pompidou [or Centre Georges Pompidou or Pompidou Center]
Democratic Republic of the Congo [or DRC or Zaire; prompt on Congo; do not accept or prompt on "Republic of the Congo"]

bonuses.leadin: This artist fired a rifle at bags filled with paint to create her “Shooting Pictures.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French artist whose recurring subjects included fertile “Nana” figures. She frequently collaborated with her husband Jean Tinguely.

Saint Phalle’s colorful sculptures face off against Tinguely’s black mechanical ones in the Stravinsky Fountain, which was installed next to this Paris art museum designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano.

Saint Phalle spent over 20 years on a public sculpture park in Tuscany inspired by these objects. Christian Bale plays a screenwriter named Rick in a 2015 Terrence Malick film which is titled for one of these objects called the Knight of Cups.
answers: Niki de Saint Phalle [or Catherine-Marie-Agnès Fal de Saint Phalle]

bonuses.leadin: The plans for this building called for three segments: a cube that rotated once per year, a pyramid that rotated once per month, and a cylinder that would rotate once a day. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this building that was planned by its namesake Constructivist architect to be built in Petrograd after the Bolshevik revolution.
Tatlin's Tower was designed to be taller than this iron lattice tower in Paris, constructed in 1889 on the Champ de Mars.
A model of Tatlin's Tower can be found in this intensely color-coded complex also in Paris, a collaborative complex designed by Richard Rogers and Renzo Piano named for a former president of France.
answers_sanitized: Tatlin's Tower [or Monument to the Third International]
Pompidou Center [or Centre Pompidou; or Centre Georges Pompidou]
